  "active_set": ["R1.1", "R1.4", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The abstract has a gap statement and contribution claim but 'make stronger' plausibly means sharpening the framing — tightening the clinical motivation, the stated gap, and the summary of what was actually demonstrated vs. merely done.",
    "R1.4": "The novelty anchor ('first cross-predictor benchmark … on a temporally held-out cohort') is present but understated; strengthening likely includes making the delta vs. prior recalibration work more salient.",
    "R1.6": "Sentence-level prose is a natural target of 'make stronger' — passive constructions, hedged phrasing, and flat clause-chaining are visible throughout and are common improvement targets in this genre."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should the primary effort go toward sharpening the scientific contribution framing and novelty claims (restructuring the gap statement and 'principal contribution' sentence), or toward improving sentence-level prose strength and rhetorical force (word choice, active voice, emphasis)?"
}

rationale.R1.1: The abstract has a gap statement and contribution claim but 'make stronger' plausibly means sharpening the framing — tightening the clinical motivation, the stated gap, and the summary of what was actually demonstrated vs. merely done.
rationale.R1.4: The novelty anchor ('first cross-predictor benchmark … on a temporally held-out cohort') is present but understated; strengthening likely includes making the delta vs. prior recalibration work more salient.
rationale.R1.6: Sentence-level prose is a natural target of 'make stronger' — passive constructions, hedged phrasing, and flat clause-chaining are visible throughout and are common improvement targets in this genre.

parsed.clarification_question: Should the primary effort go toward sharpening the scientific contribution framing and novelty claims (restructuring the gap statement and 'principal contribution' sentence), or toward improving sentence-level prose strength and rhetorical force (word choice, active voice, emphasis)?
